_doc: Latency budgets for the engine's own nightly-measurable benchmarks (Phase 66). Each budget names its owner criterion group/id under crates/krishiv-bench/benches/ so scripts/bench-tier.sh knows where to read target/criterion/<path>/new/estimates.json. The gate (scripts/bench_gate.py, ported from the platform repo's Phase 29) flags results exceeding budget by >20% on two consecutive nightly runs. Units: milliseconds unless noted. Deliberately excludes TPC-H/TPC-DS budgets: those benches need KRISHIV_TPCH_DATA_DIR_*/KRISHIV_TPCDS_DATA_DIR pointing at multi-GB generated data that CI does not provision, so they self-skip today (see BENCHMARKING.md's 'What this gate does not cover yet') rather than being declared here and permanently failing --require-fresh for an infrastructure reason, not a performance one.

budgets.note: documented target < 1ms; met at 148us in the Phase 51 yardstick
documented target < 5ms; MISSED at 11.7ms (2.3x over) in the Phase 51 yardstick, tracked for Phase 55/#195 (not yet started as of Phase 66) — expect this budget to stay red until that lands

budget borrowed from the platform repo's benchmarks/budgets.json pipeline_tick_p50 (post-G14 target) — the only recorded tick-latency SLO in either repo; feeds the Phase 64 entry-gate demand trigger (a view whose tick latency exceeds one-executor budget). Phase 51 measured 140.4ms at this point, well under budget — current data does not show the Phase 64 trigger firing
Phase 51 yardstick measured 1.61ms; budget gives ~2x headroom on shared/noisy hardware
Phase 51 yardstick measured 4.86ms; budget gives ~2x headroom on shared/noisy hardware
Phase 51 yardstick measured 3.80ms; budget gives ~2x headroom on shared/noisy hardware
Phase 51 yardstick measured 1.66ms; budget gives ~2x headroom on shared/noisy hardware
